name: Shimr ibn Thil-Jawshan
title: Embodiment of Cruelty
role: Killer of the Master of Martyrs
shortDescription: One of the most ruthless commanders of the Kufan army who prevented peace by bringing Ibn Ziyad's letter and ultimately severed Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head.
fullStory:
Shimr ibn Thil-Jawshan was a hypocritical figure in Kufa who fought alongside Amir al-Mu'minin in the Battle of Siffin and was even wounded, but later joined the Khawarij and then became a close courtier of the Umayyads.

He played a key role in the Ashura tragedy. When Umar Sa'd intended to negotiate and end the conflict peacefully, Shimr incited Ibn Ziyad and brought a threatening letter stating that if Umar Sa'd wouldn't kill the Imam, he should hand over the command to Shimr.

On the eve of Ashura, sharing tribal roots with Umm al-Banin, he brought a letter of safe conduct for Abbas (AS), which was met with a crushing response from the Moon of Bani Hashem. On Ashura, he commanded the left wing of the army.

Shimr's peak cruelty occurred on the afternoon of Ashura; he ordered the attack on the tents and ultimately entered the slaughter pit himself, unjustly severing Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head from behind. He was eventually captured by Mukhtar's men, killed, and his body was fed to dogs.
quoteArabic: يا بْنَ أَبِي تُرابٍ! أَلَسْتَ تَزْعُمُ أَنَّ أَبَاكَ ساقِي الحَوْضِ... فَاصْبِرْ حَتّى يَسْقِيَكَ...
quote: (His insulting mockery to the Imam:) O son of Abu Turab! Don't you claim your father serves the Pond of Kawthar? Then wait until he quenches your thirst!
